data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== DSO re-explore (from-scratch): preplanning lossless + reconcile
=== agent a1b3f72696b6ff5b6

All citations confirmed. I have everything needed for a complete, lossless extraction. Let me compile the final report.

---

# LOSSLESS RE-EXTRACTION — DSO Preplanning Skill Evaluation/QC Dimensions

Root: `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`. All line numbers verified against the actual files. Files read in full: `skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` (1342 lines), `docs/review-criteria.md`, all 6 `docs/reviewers/*.md`, all 4 `prompts/*.md`, `agents/story-decomposer.md`, `agents/red-team-reviewer.md`, `agents/blue-team-filter.md`.

---

## SECTION 1 — ARCHITECTURE BOARD: 6 reviewers, EVERY dimension, BOTH rubric columns

**Shared framing** (`docs/review-criteria.md`): 6-specialist virtual board reviews every story via `/dso:review-protocol` Stage 1 (per-story). `pass_threshold: 4` — ALL dimension scores must be 4, 5, or null (N/A) to pass; any score <4 fails the story for that dimension (`review-criteria.md:10, :47-48`). Launched in parallel via Task tool, `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` (`:30, :38`). All output conforms to `REVIEW-SCHEMA.md`. Max 3 automated revision cycles, then escalate to user (`:50`). Caller schema hash `dba581aa06265af0` (`:96`). Where invoked: `docs/review-criteria.md` is the protocol; the per-story board is distinct from the **batch** Phase C Risk & Scope Scan in `SKILL.md:483-529` which references the SAME 6 reviewer files but as a lightweight no-sub-agent scan ("no sub-agent dispatch, no scored review, no revision cycles" — `SKILL.md:485`).

Universal 1-5 scale (identical in all 6 files): 5 Exceptional/production-ready · 4 Strong/minor polish · 3 Adequate/notable gaps · 2 Needs Work/significant issues · 1 Unacceptable/fundamental redesign · N/A Not Applicable.

### 1.1 SECURITY — `docs/reviewers/security.md` (Joe-flagged)
Persona: Senior Security Engineer; OWASP Top 10 baseline; defense-in-depth (`security.md:1-6`). Each finding below 4 MUST cite an OWASP category and actionable remediation (`:39-41`); domain field `owasp_category` (`:57-58`).

| Dim | file:line | "4 or 5" (literal) | "below 4" (literal) | scoring |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **access_classification** | `security.md:23` | "Every new endpoint and data access path explicitly declares its intended access level: public (no auth, documented rationale), authenticated (auth required, roles/scopes specified), or internal-only (not exposed to external traffic). Endpoints that read or modify user data, PII, or sensitive resources require authentication; public endpoints are limited to non-sensitive read operations and their public status is a deliberate, documented choice — not an omission" | "New endpoints or data access paths with no declared access level — the reader cannot tell whether the endpoint is intentionally public or the auth requirement was forgotten. Or: endpoints that handle sensitive data (user records, PII, uploaded documents, credentials) are exposed without authentication. The key failure is ambiguity, not that auth is absent — a clearly documented public endpoint scores well; an undeclared endpoint scores poorly" | 1-5; never null. Explicit anti-inflation: undeclared-access new endpoint = **score ≤2 regardless of other quality** (`:42-44`) |
| **data_protection** | `security.md:24` | "Sensitive data is encrypted at rest and in transit; PII/secrets are identified and handled per policy; no data leakage paths in scope" | "Sensitive data transmitted or stored without encryption; PII handling unaddressed; logging of secrets possible" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if "story introduces no new data storage, transmission, or PII handling whatsoever" (`:36-37`) |

Overlap: `access_classification` overlaps maintainability `coupling_risk` (auth boundary = cross-component dependency) and testing `user_journey_coverage` (caller contract). `data_protection` overlaps reliability `failover` (durable state). Conflict precedent: security "restrict to owner" vs performance "cache globally" = `strict_vs_flexible` (`review-criteria.md:61`).

### 1.2 ACCESSIBILITY — `docs/reviewers/accessibility.md` (Joe-flagged)
Persona: WCAG Accessibility Specialist; WCAG 2.1 AA; inclusive UX (`accessibility.md:1-7`). Below-4 finding MUST cite WCAG 2.1 SC + actionable remediation for DDs/scope (`:40-43`); domain field `wcag_criterion` (`:57-58`).

| Dim | file:line | "4 or 5" (literal) | "below 4" (literal) | scoring |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **wcag_compliance** | `accessibility.md:24` | "Story scope explicitly addresses WCAG 2.1 AA; done definitions include observable accessibility outcomes (keyboard navigable, screen-reader compatible, sufficient contrast); no known compliance gaps" | "Story introduces new UI without any accessibility requirements; WCAG criteria unaddressed; success criteria describe only visual or pointer-based interactions" | 1-5 or null; null for BOTH dims if story introduces no user-facing UI (purely backend/infra/data-processing) (`:37-38`). Anti-inflation: new interactive page without keyboard-nav reqs = **score of 2** (`:43-45`) |
| **inclusive_ux** | `accessibility.md:25` | "Story scope accounts for reduced motion, keyboard-only users, and screen reader users; touch targets and cognitive load considered in done definitions" | "Story assumes mouse-only interaction; relies on color alone to convey meaning; no consideration for reduced motion or high contrast preferences" | 1-5 or null (same null rule as wcag_compliance) |

Overlap: both overlap testing `user_journey_coverage`/`verifiable_outcomes` (observable user-visible outcomes) and the UI-designer dispatch's review-protocol "Accessibility" perspective (`ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md:188-194`: `contrast_hierarchy`, `interaction_a11y`). Conflict precedent: accessibility "add detailed WCAG DDs" vs maintainability "scope too prescriptive" = `more_vs_less` (`review-criteria.md:63`).

### 1.3 PERFORMANCE — `docs/reviewers/performance.md`
Persona: Senior Performance Engineer; observable latency + sustainable load (`performance.md:1-8`). Below-4: describe risk, estimate impact (Big-O), concrete mitigation (`:45-47`); domain field `impact_estimate` (`:62-64`).

| Dim | file:line | "4 or 5" (literal) | "below 4" (literal) | scoring |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **latency** | `performance.md:24` | "Done definitions include observable time-bounded outcomes (e.g., \"within 30 seconds\"); story scope avoids synchronous blocking calls on hot paths" | "No latency targets defined; story requires synchronous processing where async would serve; user-facing wait times unaddressed" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no user-facing/time-sensitive ops (`:38-40`). Anti-inflation: no latency target for user-facing op = **score of 2** (`:48-49`) |
| **resource_efficiency** | `performance.md:25` | "Story scope avoids N+1 query patterns, redundant API calls, and unbounded memory growth; batch processing considered where applicable" | "Story implies N+1 queries, redundant LLM calls, or processes entire datasets in memory without pagination or streaming" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no new data access, LLM calls, or compute paths (`:40-41`) |
| **scalability** | `performance.md:26` | "Story defines behavior at input size boundaries (e.g., maximum document size, page count limits, or graceful degradation for oversized inputs); concurrent access patterns are addressed — connection pools, external API rate limits, queue depth, and thread/worker pool capacity are considered when the story introduces new I/O paths or shared resources; done definitions include load expectations (e.g., \"supports 10 concurrent uploads\") or explicitly state single-user scope" | "Story introduces new processing paths with no consideration of input size limits — a 500-page document follows the same code path as a 5-page document with no guardrails; no mention of concurrent access behavior when the feature involves shared resources (database connections, LLM API quotas, file storage); story assumes single-user operation without stating this as a deliberate constraint" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no new processing paths AND no new shared-resource consumption (`:41-43`) |

Overlap: `scalability` boundary-input checks overlap testing `boundary_scenarios` (size/volume extremes); `resource_efficiency` overlaps reliability `error_handling` (external calls). Conflict precedents: performance "batch ops" vs reliability "per-item retry/backoff" = `expand_vs_reduce` (`review-criteria.md:64`); testing "mock LLM" vs reliability "live circuit breaker" = `add_vs_remove` (`:62`).

### 1.4 TESTING — `docs/reviewers/testing.md`
Persona: Senior Software Engineer in Test; "testable by construction" (`testing.md:1-8`). Below-4: describe gap + concrete DD/scope addition framed as user/consumer-visible outcome (`:45-47`); domain field `affected_path` (`:70-72`).

| Dim | file:line | "4 or 5" (literal) | "below 4" (literal) | scoring |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **user_journey_coverage** | `testing.md:25` | "Story identifies the critical user paths it introduces or modifies — both success and failure experiences. Done definitions describe what the user sees when things go wrong (upload fails, processing times out, input is invalid, service is unavailable), not just the happy path. For backend stories: the consumer-facing contract is defined (what does the caller receive on success, on error, on timeout?)" | "Story describes only the happy path; no mention of what the user sees when an operation fails, times out, or receives invalid input. Error states are left implicit (\"the system handles errors gracefully\" with no definition of what \"gracefully\" means). Backend stories define no caller-facing contract for failure cases" | 1-5 or null; null for ALL dims only if no user/consumer-facing behavior (`:40-43`). Anti-inflation: new user-facing flow with only happy-path AC = **score of 2** (`:56-57`) |
| **boundary_scenarios** | `testing.md:26` | "Story considers realistic but non-obvious user behaviors and input extremes: navigation interruptions (back button, tab close, page refresh mid-operation), input diversity (non-Latin characters, very long strings, special characters, empty submissions), size and volume boundaries (oversized files, bulk operations, zero-item cases), and environment variations (slow connections, mobile viewports) relevant to the feature's scope. Done definitions address at least the most likely boundary scenarios for each new user-facing interaction. For backend stories: consumer-side boundary inputs are considered (malformed payloads, missing fields, unexpected content types)" | "Story only considers the expected input and expected user behavior; no consideration of what happens when users deviate from the intended flow — back button during multi-step process, oversized input, unexpected characters, or interrupted operations. Boundary behavior is left entirely to the implementer's judgment" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no new user inputs/uploads/interactive flows (`:42-43`) |
| **verifiable_outcomes** | `testing.md:27` | "Done definitions describe observable, measurable outcomes that can be verified without ambiguity — \"user sees an error message with the failed file name\" rather than \"errors are handled correctly\"; \"page loads within 3 seconds\" rather than \"page is fast\". Each acceptance criterion has a clear pass/fail signal that a test (manual or automated) could evaluate" | "Done definitions use vague language: \"works correctly\", \"handles errors gracefully\", \"performs well\". Outcomes are not observable — they describe internal state (\"data is saved to the database\") rather than user-visible behavior (\"user sees a confirmation message\"). A tester reading the story cannot determine what to verify" | 1-5 or null (same null rule) |

Overlap: `verifiable_outcomes` overlaps the DD-quality `vague` micro-check (`SKILL.md:916`) and maintainability — but at story-DD-vocabulary level; `boundary_scenarios` overlaps performance `scalability`; `user_journey_coverage` overlaps reliability `error_handling`.

### 1.5 RELIABILITY — `docs/reviewers/reliability.md`
Persona: Senior Site Reliability Engineer; fail-safe/recover-predictably/no-cascade (`reliability.md:1-6`). Below-4: describe failure mode, blast radius, concrete mitigation (`:40-44`); domain field `blast_radius` (`:68-70`).

| Dim | file:line | "4 or 5" (literal) | "below 4" (literal) | scoring |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **error_handling** | `reliability.md:23` | "Story scope addresses how new failure points are handled: circuit breakers, retry logic with backoff, or graceful degradation to a known-good state; error states surfaced to users where appropriate" | "Story introduces new failure points (LLM calls, external integrations, file I/O) with no error handling mentioned; system behavior on failure is undefined; no graceful degradation" | 1-5 or null. Anti-inflation: external API call with no error handling = **score of 2** (`:43-44`) |
| **failover** | `reliability.md:24` | "Story scope includes or explicitly defers recovery behavior; system can resume a failed operation without data loss or corruption; partial-progress state is durable or safely discarded" | "Story creates state that could be left corrupted on failure; retry of a failed operation produces inconsistent results; no idempotency guarantee for write operations" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no write ops/stateful transitions/external deps that could leave partial state (`:36-38`) |
| **blast_radius** (TIEBREAKER, not a scored dimension) | `reliability.md:46-56` | n/a — "Use blast radius as a tiebreaker, not an override." (`:46`) Narrow (single user/request) → score on failure mode alone; Moderate (category of users / shared resource like job queue) → "subtract 1 from what the failure mode alone would score, minimum 1" (`:50`); Wide (all concurrent users / data corruption across records / cascading failure) → "subtract 1 ... minimum 1" (`:51`). "Blast radius never raises a score" (`:53`); a trivial failure with wide blast radius does not become blocking (`:53-56`) | — | ONLY LOWERS (min 1); never raises |

Overlap: `failover` overlaps security `data_protection` (durable state) and performance `scalability` (concurrent shared resources); `error_handling` overlaps testing `user_journey_coverage` (failure-path experience).

### 1.6 MAINTAINABILITY — `docs/reviewers/maintainability.md`
Persona: Senior Software Architect; "easy or expensive to change six months from now?" (`maintainability.md:1-7`). Below-4: describe risk + concrete DD/scope addition (`:46-48`); domain field `affected_docs` (`:64-66`).

| Dim | file:line | "4 or 5" (literal) | "below 4" (literal) | scoring |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **coupling_risk** | `maintainability.md:24` | "Story acknowledges and manages new cross-component dependencies it introduces. If the story connects previously independent modules, services, or data stores, the scope identifies the coupling and either justifies it or includes a mitigation (e.g., an interface boundary, an event rather than a direct call). Stories that operate within a single existing module or extend an existing integration point without adding new dependencies score well" | "Story introduces new dependencies between previously independent components with no acknowledgment — a new service calls directly into another service's internals, a shared database table is read by a module that previously had no DB dependency, or a cross-cutting change touches multiple modules without identifying the coupling. The reader cannot tell whether the coupling is intentional or accidental" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if entirely within single existing module, no new cross-component interaction (`:38-39`) |
| **changeability** | `maintainability.md:25` | "Story avoids locking in assumptions that are likely to evolve. Business rules that may change are scoped as configurable rather than hardcoded; data models leave room for known future variations (e.g., \"currently supports PDF only\" explicitly acknowledges the constraint rather than embedding it invisibly); integration boundaries are defined so that swapping a provider or changing a workflow step does not require rewriting unrelated code" | "Story hardcodes business rules, thresholds, or provider-specific logic that the domain is known to evolve (e.g., embedding LLM prompt templates as string literals with no extraction path, hardcoding file type restrictions deep in processing logic rather than at a configuration boundary). Design assumes current requirements are permanent with no acknowledgment of known change vectors" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no business rules/thresholds/integration points (e.g., pure UI text change) (`:40-41`) |
| **documentation** | `maintainability.md:26` | "Story done definitions include updating relevant documentation for non-obvious decisions (e.g., ADRs for new architectural patterns, CLAUDE.md for new pipeline stages, `DESIGN.md` for new UI patterns); pure implementation stories with no novel decisions score null" | "Story introduces a new architectural pattern, API contract, or pipeline stage with no documentation requirement; future agents will not know why decisions were made" | 1-5 or null; null ONLY if no architectural decisions/new patterns/contract updates (`:41-44`). Anti-inflation: new pipeline stage with no doc requirement = **score of 2** (`:49-51`) |

Overlap: `coupling_risk` overlaps security `access_classification` (boundaries) and red-team taxonomy cat-1 `implicit_shared_state` / cat-3 `dependency_gap`; `documentation` overlaps the SKILL Documentation-Update-Story doc-router gates (`SKILL.md:968-993`).

---

## SECTION 2 — SC→DD COVERAGE AUDIT (buckets + 3-condition standard)

This dimension appears in **THREE places** with intentional redundancy (`SKILL.md:477`: "the two audits are intentionally redundant — decomposition is the constructive step, the red team is the adversarial check"):

**(A) Constructive — story-decomposer** (`agents/story-decomposer.md:197-208`, Step 1 "Coverage Map"). For every SC AND every CC, classify into exactly one bucket (verified literal):
- `covered_by_existing` (`:203`): "One or more existing/external-dep stories already produce the SC's outcome (same observable outcome, same scope, same measurability). No new story needed."
- `partial_coverage` (`:204`): "An existing story addresses the SC but its DD scope is narrower than the SC requires. A new story or DD extension is needed to close the gap."
- `uncovered` (`:205`): "No existing story produces the SC's outcome. A new story is required."
- `out_of_scope_for_stories` (`:206`): "The SC is structural to the epic itself (a constraint, not a deliverable a story can complete) and belongs at the epic level. Note in your output but do not draft a story for it."
- **3-condition standard** (`:208`): "an existing story 'covers' an SC only when all three are true (same observable outcome, same scope, same measurability). When in doubt, classify as `partial_coverage`."
- CC coverage is **symmetric** to SC, recorded in parallel `cc_coverage_plan` (`:199`); "A DD covers a CC when its evidence demonstrates the durable invariant the CC names." Output schema `story-decomposer.md:285-393`; `gap_summary` required when verdict ∈ {partial_coverage, uncovered, out_of_scope_for_stories} (`:380, :391`).

**(B) Adversarial — red-team-reviewer story_review** (`agents/red-team-reviewer.md:150-176`, "mandatory and runs before the interaction gap taxonomy"). Buckets differ in naming (`fully_covered`/`partially_covered` vs decomposer's `covered_by_existing`/`partial_coverage`):
- `fully_covered` (`:159`): "One or more story DDs explicitly produce the SC's outcome. No finding emitted."
- `partially_covered` (`:160`): "A story DD addresses the SC but its scope, conditions, or measurability is narrower than the SC requires (a summarization weakening). Emit a `modify_done_definition` finding ... `taxonomy_category: \"sc_coverage_gap\"`."
- `uncovered` (`:161`): "No story DD produces the SC's outcome. Emit a `new_story` finding ... `taxonomy_category: \"sc_coverage_gap\"`."
- `out_of_scope_for_stories` (`:162`): "structural to the epic itself ... Emit an `escalate_to_epic` finding ... `taxonomy_category: \"sc_coverage_gap\"`."
- **3-condition Coverage Standard** (`red-team-reviewer.md:164-171`, literal): "A story DD 'covers' an SC only when all three are true: [1] The DD produces the same observable outcome (not a related one, not a precursor). [2] The DD's scope matches or exceeds the SC's scope (no narrowing of conditions, users, data shapes, or environments). [3] The DD is measurable in the same terms the SC is measurable in (a vague DD does not cover a specific SC). If any of the three fails, the SC is `partially_covered`, not `fully_covered`."
- Mandatory `sc_coverage_summary` block ALWAYS emitted regardless of gaps — "Omitting it is a protocol violation" (`:174-175, :331-342`).

**(C) Orchestrator re-validation** of decomposer output (`SKILL.md:445-451`): `sc_coverage_plan` one-entry-per-SC, field presence, `gap_summary` condition (`:447`); every uncovered/partial SC referenced by ≥1 draft DD `← Satisfies:` annotation (`:449`); no dangling `depends_on` (`:450`).

Scoring: bucket enum (not 1-5). Where used: decomposer Phase = Story Decomposition; red-team = Phase E Step 1 (`phase-e-adversarial-review.md:9-39`); orchestrator parse = SKILL Parse-Output. Overlap: This is the core dimension the catalog identifies as "the inverse of completion-verifier's aspirational-implementation detector."

---

## SECTION 3 — INVEST + vertical slicing

Appears in **two places**:

**(A) SKILL Phase F** (`SKILL.md:648-664`):
- INVEST (`:650`, literal): "Each story must satisfy **INVEST** — Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small (one sub-agent session), Testable. For any story that fails one principle: add dependencies/split (I), remove implementation details (N), combine with others (V), add context (E), split (S), or add specific acceptance criteria (T)." — note **"Small (one sub-agent session)"** is the DSO-specific Small definition.
- Vertical Slicing (`:652-664`): Good = "User can upload a PDF and see extraction results"; Bad (horizontal) = "Create database schema for documents" / "Build document upload API" / "Add frontend upload component"; "The vertical slice includes all layers necessary to deliver value."

**(B) story-decomposer Steps 3-4** (`story-decomposer.md:214-229`): Step 3 vertical slices same good/bad examples (`:216-219`); Step 4 INVEST with per-letter self-check (`:222-229`) — I=add explicit `depends_on`, N=strip "use Redis"/"with Foo library" unless epic constraint, V=combine with consumer, E=add scope clarifications, S="One sub-agent session can complete it. If too large, split — explicitly flag with `split_candidate: true`", T="Done Definitions are specific enough that `/dso:implementation-plan` can derive `Verify:` commands."

Scoring: not numeric — gate/self-check producing fix actions or `split_candidate: true`. Overlap: S overlaps complexity-evaluator (catalog Part 3.8); T overlaps story-decomposer verify_commands + DD-quality checks. Foundation/Enhancement split is a distinct sub-mechanism (next).

---

## SECTION 4 — Foundation/Enhancement Splitting (Phase F Step 4)

`SKILL.md:666-699`. Decision question (`:670`): "Does the minimum that delivers the functional goal differ significantly from the ideal experience or architecture?" Definitions: **Foundation** (`:672`) = "Delivers the functional goal and proves the concept. This IS the walking skeleton slice for the story"; **Enhancement** (`:673`) = "Invests in the ideal experience — better UX, proper architecture, performance optimization. Depends on Foundation." **Split if** (`:675-678`): Foundation alone delivers user value (complete vertical slice) AND Enhancement is meaningful scope increase (not polish) AND combined too large for one agent session. **Don't split if** (`:680-683`): Foundation wouldn't deliver value without Enhancement / scope difference marginal / already small enough. Foundation higher priority; `depends_on` Enhancement→Foundation; both trace same epic criterion (`:693-697`). Precedence guard: `splitRole: Foundation|Enhancement` marker makes preplanning's split authoritative; ui-designer `scope_split_proposals` skipped (`SKILL.md:699`; enforced `ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md:317-340`). Scoring: gate (split / don't split). Walking Skeleton itself = `SKILL.md:640-646` ("absolute minimum end-to-end path"); decomposer Step 2 (`story-decomposer.md:210-212`).

---

## SECTION 5 — Adversarial red-team story_review: 8-category interaction taxonomy + blue-team filters

### 5.1 Red-team story_review taxonomy (`agents/red-team-reviewer.md`)
NOTE on the "8-category" framing: the doc header (`:18`) and `taxonomy_category` enum (`:329`) enumerate **8 values** = `sc_coverage_gap` PLUS 7 interaction categories. The interaction-gap prose section is titled with 7 numbered categories (`:186-240`). The frontmatter description says "8 taxonomy categories" (`:4`) but Mode line `:18` says "7 taxonomy categories" for the interaction taxonomy alone. So: **8 total enum values = 1 coverage + 7 interaction.**

Full `taxonomy_category` enum (`red-team-reviewer.md:329`, literal): `sc_coverage_gap`, `implicit_shared_state`, `conflicting_assumptions`, `dependency_gap`, `scope_overlap`, `ordering_violation`, `consumer_impact`, `residual_references`.

The 7 interaction categories (each: "systematically check every story individually AND every pair of stories for interactions" `:188`):
1. **Implicit Shared State** (`:190-195`): stories read/write same state (DB tables, config, session data, UI components) without explicit dependency; exclusive-ownership assumptions; same workflow uncoordinated; same infra resource (queue/cache/external service).
2. **Conflicting Assumptions** (`:197-203`): incompatible assumptions — field optional vs required; contradictory UX flows for same action; different auth/authz models; different output formats/data shapes for shared interfaces.
3. **Dependency Gaps** (`:205-210`): missing deps causing wrong-order failures — consumes another story's output with no declared dep; assumes infra (table/endpoint/config key) created by another story; natural impl order contradicts declared graph.
4. **Scope Overlaps** (`:212-217`): ambiguous/overlapping boundaries — two stories claim same functional area; "out of scope" for one not "in scope" for any (gap); same observable outcome in different terms.
5. **Ordering and Sequencing Violations** (`:219-224`): temporal ordering not in deps — considerations reference risks existing only after another story; must-deploy-in-order with no dep; assumes migration/schema change already applied.
6. **Consumer Impact / Operational Readiness** (`:226-232`): create/modify systems consumed elsewhere without verifying consumers — changes data format/API contract/file structure without testing downstream; assumes consumer "just works" with no integration verification; scope excludes updating consumers but breaks them; migration verifies data integrity but not functional integrity.
7. **Residual References** (`:234-240`): deprecate/relocate/rename shared resource but fail to find all references/consumers — rename config key/file path/function/endpoint without enumerating callers; move module without updating import paths/symlinks/docs; remove public interface without checking consumers; migration for one consumer leaves others on deprecated resource.

Supporting machinery: **Consumer Enumeration** before analysis (`:177-184`: enumerate all scripts/hooks/skills/tests referencing the system, note path + reference type + whether epic affects it; used for Category 6). **Analysis Instructions** (`:242-251`): coverage audit first; per-category check every story + every pair; "Only report high-confidence, actionable findings -- do not include speculative warnings or theoretical concerns" (`:246`); each finding → concrete remediation (new story / modified DD / new dependency / new consideration) (`:247`); skip empty categories, "do not fabricate findings to appear thorough" (`:248`); cross-reference Risk Register to avoid Phase-2 duplication (`:250`). Finding `type` enum: `new_story | modify_done_definition | add_dependency | add_consideration | escalate_to_epic` (`:324`). Model gate: opus required, else `{"findings": [], "error": "model_requirement_unmet"}` (`:12`).

**inference_challenge mode** (`red-team-reviewer.md:21-124`) — a SEPARATE mode (NOT story_review): evaluates PRECONDITIONS decisions_log for inferred-vs-explicit sourcing (`:32-42`); sampling tiers (`:43-65`): **Always challenge** (100%) when `affects_fields` includes `gate_verdicts` OR `workflow_completion_checklist` (`:49`); **Probabilistic** (20%) all other rationale-text-only via `hash_bucket = int(SHA-256(decision_id)[-8:],16) mod 5`, challenge if `==0` (`:50, :52-63`). NEVER returns silence — always `INFERENCE_CHALLENGE` or `INFERENCE_SKIP` (`:30`). Scoring: per-decision verdict enum.

### 5.2 Blue-team filter (`agents/blue-team-filter.md`) — sonnet
ALL 4 criteria must pass for a finding to survive (`:28`):
1. **Actionable** (`:30-35`): reject vague warnings without clear fix, theoretical risks requiring speculation about future requirements, already-standard-practice recommendations.
2. **Real Cross-Story Interaction** (`:37-42`): reject single-story concerns already in that story's DDs/considerations, risks already in Phase C risk register, gaps within one story's internal scope (implementation-plan-level not preplanning-level).
3. **Distinct from Existing Considerations** (`:44-49`): reject duplicates of existing consideration, restatements of existing DD, dependency already in graph.
4. **High Confidence** (`:51-57`): reject findings assuming a specific technical approach the story leaves open, predicting failure modes depending on undecided impl details, extrapolating from general SWE concerns rather than this story map.

**Fail-open / when-in-doubt-accept** (the anti-false-negative bias): "If you cannot evaluate a specific finding ... **pass it through** ... Err on the side of inclusion when uncertain. This is a fail-open policy for individual findings." (`:58-60`); Rules: "When in doubt about a finding, **accept it** -- false negatives (missing a real gap) are worse than false positives (flagging a non-issue) at this stage" (`:139`); for `sc_coverage_gap` specifically: "apply the 'when in doubt, accept' rule with extra weight" because rejecting one leaves an epic SC uncovered (`:107`). Output adds `disposition` (accept|reject) + `rejection_rationale` (`:100-103`); accepted in `findings[]`, rejected in `rejected[]`; never writes files, `artifact_path: null` (`:127-129`). Scoring: per-finding accept/reject.

### 5.3 Phase E orchestration wrapper (`prompts/phase-e-adversarial-review.md` + `SKILL.md:558-607`)
Trigger: ≥3 stories after Phase C; skipped under `--lightweight` (`:1-7`). Red→blue dispatch (`:9-56`); validation ORDER pinned: model_requirement_unmet check FIRST, schema validation SECOND (`:27-36`). Blue-team failure → discard ALL unfiltered findings, proceed (`:56`). Remediation re-dispatch loop (Step 4, `:92-156`): by-reference `remediation_context` (artifact absolute paths + `target_story_ids`), story-decomposer DELTA OUTPUT mode with pre-generation Read gate + DD-superset preservation. Two null-case guards preserved separately: (a) PRE-filter red-team-zero skips Steps 2-4; (b) POST-filter blue-team-rejected-all skips only Step 4 (`:98-103`). Loop governance (`SKILL.md:565-607`): `MAX_CYCLES` from `get_max_remediation_cycles()`; per-cycle `Current cycle: N of MAX_CYCLES`; oscillation-check hard gate at cycle≥2; `REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm` IFF `cycle_count==MAX_CYCLES AND findings non-empty`; terminal tokens `HALT_FOR_USER`/`OSCILLATION_HALT`/`PROTOCOL_ERROR`.

## SECTION 6 — DD-quality micro-checks (compound / vague / spec-fidelity / scoping-assumption)

All in `SKILL.md` story authoring (Phase H Step 2), enforced by external scripts:

| Check | name | what | literal | file:line | scoring | overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **DD Lexicon — compound** | `compound` | DD contains ` AND ` joining two requirements → split into two DDs | "A **compound** violation (type `compound`) means the DD contains ` AND ` joining two requirements — split into two separate done definitions." | `SKILL.md:916` (script `check-dd-lexicon.sh`, loop `:903-913`) | gate (exit≠0 → HALT story, `:907-912`) | overlaps task-design atomicity / `more_vs_less` conflicts |
| **DD Lexicon — vague** | `vague` | imprecise language (properly/correctly/should) → replace with observable/measurable | "A **vague** violation (type `vague`) means the DD uses imprecise language (e.g., `properly`, `correctly`, `should`) — replace with observable, measurable language." | `SKILL.md:916` | gate | overlaps testing `verifiable_outcomes` (`testing.md:27`) |
| **Spec-Fidelity** | spec-fidelity (PIL→DD ≥80% word-match) | verify PIL criteria preserved in DDs; ≥80% of a criterion's words must appear in any DD line | "A PIL criterion is considered matched when ≥80% of its words appear in any DD line (near-match). If a criterion has no match, the script emits JSON `{\"type\":\"drop\",\"pil_text\":\"<criterion>\",\"dd_matches\":[]}` on stderr and exits 1. **Do not auto-correct** — surface the mismatch to the user and halt story finalization." | `SKILL.md:918-938` (script `spec-fidelity-check.sh`) | gate (exit 1 → halt, show diff, NO auto-correct, `:931-934, :938`) | overlaps SC→DD coverage (both detect summarization drop); distinct in keying on PIL criteria not SCs |
| **Scoping-Assumption** | Verify Scoping Assumptions | every "OUT" assertion claiming X exists/handled-elsewhere needs a `Verify:` command run to exit 0 | "After writing the Scope section for each story, verify every \"OUT\" assertion that claims something already exists or is handled elsewhere: 1. ... write a `Verify:` command that confirms the assertion 2. Run the command. If it fails, the assumption is wrong — either move the item to IN scope or add a dependency ... 3. Document verified assumptions inline: `OUT: [item] — Verified: [command] returned exit 0`" + "Why this matters: False preconditions encoded as scoping decisions are invisible to downstream validation." | `SKILL.md:1302-1310` | gate (fail → move to IN or add dep) | NEW class — overlaps red-team Category 4 `scope_overlap` ("out of scope for one not in scope for any") |

Additional DD-quality items at the same altitude:
- **SC Contradiction Check** (`SKILL.md:878-891`): DD contradicts its `← Satisfies:` SC if "DD's observable outcome, if fully achieved, would leave the SC unsatisfied"; patterns: Bypass annotation, Partial coverage, Scope narrowing (`:882-884`). Revise DD (SC authoritative) or flag SC to user; "Do NOT proceed with stories whose DDs contradict their SCs — this produces plans that are structurally guaranteed to fail the completion verifier" (`:889`). `← Validates Closure Check:` form exempt (`:891`).
- **TDD Done-of-Done** (`SKILL.md:893-897`): code-change stories MUST include "'unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic'" DD; doc/research/non-code exempt.
- **Verify-command negative-constraint list** (decomposer `story-decomposer.md:240, :274`): a verify command is invalid if it matches any of `grep, find, ls, wc, cat, head, stat, test -f, test -e, [ -f, [ -e, file , du , diff ` — "If the only way to verify a DD is via file inspection, the DD is not behavioral — revise it."
- **LIVE-VERIFIED SC → env-isolated integration-test DD** (`story-decomposer.md:244-258`): any LIVE-VERIFIED SC forces ≥1 story to include a config-isolated integration-test DD; detection heuristics list `:252-256`.
